V-M-052.purpose: Verify that the official MTProxy data-plane is reachable through KrotPN public 443 and proves real Telegram protocol availability without leaking secrets.

module-checks.check-2: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_deploy_phase38_static.py tests/test_domain_tls_edge_static.py tests/test_mtproto_de_edge_contract.py -v
module-checks.check-3: docker compose config with Phase-40 MTProxy services enabled, using redacted env fixtures only.

scenarios.scenario-1: Launcher builds official mtproto-proxy arguments from active 32-hex secrets and passes them as -S values without echoing secrets.
scenarios.scenario-2: Runtime stats and health are bound privately or to loopback and expose no user secrets, tg links, or runtime tokens.
scenarios.scenario-3: RU public 443 routes HTTPS ClientHello for krotpn.xyz to web and routes non-HTTPS MTProto traffic to DE official MTProxy.
scenarios.scenario-4: Live req_pq through the public KrotPN endpoint receives resPQ for the issued secure secret.
scenarios.scenario-5: Official config fetch failure uses documented cached files or marks runtime degraded without returning active user payloads.
scenarios.scenario-6: Invalid or empty secret manifest prevents official runtime promotion and emits redacted degraded status.
scenarios.scenario-7: Any web HTTPS, backend API, or admin 8443 regression blocks release readiness.

redaction-rules.rule-1: Never print raw 32-hex secrets, dd-prefixed secrets, full tg links, proxy-secret file contents, runtime tokens, wildcard private keys, JWTs, API keys, or raw .env content.
redaction-rules.rule-2: Allowed evidence is assignment id, masked hostname, secret fingerprint, active secret count, route state, req_pq pass/fail, and high-level container health.

evidence-levels.level-1: Deterministic static assertions first: shell syntax, config rendering, routing rules, private stats binding, and secret-output grep guards.
evidence-levels.level-2: Integration checks second: compose config, container command inspection, HAProxy route classification, and rollback path.
evidence-levels.level-3: Live proof last: public RU 443 req_pq and operator Telegram Desktop/mobile availability confirmation.

failure-policy.policy-1: Any req_pq timeout keeps Phase-40 blocked-live.
failure-policy.policy-2: Any raw secret or full proxy link in logs, docker output, admin output, or smoke output fails verification.
failure-policy.policy-3: Any regression in web HTTPS, backend API, or admin 8443 fails release readiness.
